page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4n server family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, which yields a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPU hardware accelerators. It also does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Network and storage operations rely entirely on attached network-allocated block storage and standard virtual network interfaces.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c4n-highcpu-16 is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but have modest memory footprints. This hardware profile is suitable for tasks such as batch processing, distributed compute nodes, web serving, and CPU-bound application servers. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, offering a predictable cost-to-performance ratio for compute-intensive deployments.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance from the c4n family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per virtual core, making it suitable for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory allocations. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage needs must be met via attached network volumes. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the c4n-highcpu-16 delivers predictable processing power for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed computing nodes. It offers a resource-dense profile for environments prioritizing raw compute capacity over memory or local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for CPU-intensive workloads and batch processing.
